remediation.executive_summary: Cross-diagram incident affecting 1 topology domain(s) (app_db_topology). 4 alert event(s) detected. Primary suspected root cause: DB-MASTER in app_db_topology. Immediate triage recommended to validate and restore service.
evidence_from_graph: Alert propagation path: DB-MASTER → APP-01 → APP-02 → DB-REPLICA-01
Impacted nodes: DB-MASTER, APP-01, APP-02, DB-REPLICA-01
CE-001 (temporal_correlation, confidence=1.0): 4 alert(s) within a 12-minute window (t=0..12 min), consistent with a propagating fault.
CE-002 (alert_sequence, confidence=0.85): Alert type sequence matches propagation chain: cpu -> latency. This ordering is consistent with fault propagation from the initiating node.

CE-003 (topology_proximity, confidence=0.925): Alerted nodes (DB-MASTER, APP-01, APP-02, DB-REPLICA-01) are topologically proximate (topology score 0.92), suggesting fault propagation through connected infrastructure.
CE-004 (propagation_hypothesis, confidence=0.9625): Hypothesis: fault initiated at DB-MASTER (alert: cpu_spike) and propagated to 1 downstream node(s) matching known propagation patterns.
Correlation reason: Propagation pattern detected: cpu -> latency
Correlation reason: Alerted nodes are directly adjacent (1-2 hops)
pre_checks: Confirm root-cause candidate DB-MASTER is present in enterprise graph memory.
Validate impacted diagrams before any change: app_db_topology.
Confirm RCA source and alert timeline freshness before remediation.
Run read-only reachability checks across diagram boundaries first.
triage_steps: Focus initial investigation on node 'DB-MASTER' in app_db_topology.
Check application server error logs for connection-refused or timeout patterns.
Verify database connection pool utilisation and active session count.
Confirm load balancer backend health checks are passing.
Review recent application deployments or configuration changes.
validation_steps: Confirm alert frequency has decreased after initial triage actions.
Verify that impact-path nodes are returning to healthy state across all diagrams.
Run connectivity tests across affected diagram boundaries.
Confirm monitoring system shows green health on root-cause node.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: DB-MASTER, APP-01, APP-02, DB-REPLICA-01.
remediation_steps: Restore 'DB-MASTER' (app_db_topology) by restarting the affected service or applying corrective configuration.
Re-advertise affected routes or re-establish BGP/OSPF sessions if routing is impacted.
Verify that downstream nodes in the impact path have recovered.
Clear alert conditions on monitoring platform and confirm no secondary triggers.
Document the change applied and update the CMDB record for the affected device.
post_checks: Confirm alert frequency has decreased after initial triage actions.
Verify that impact-path nodes are returning to healthy state across all diagrams.
Run connectivity tests across affected diagram boundaries.
Confirm monitoring system shows green health on root-cause node.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: DB-MASTER, APP-01, APP-02, DB-REPLICA-01.
do_not_execute_if: Do not execute changes if root cause DB-MASTER is not confirmed by graph evidence.
Do not execute if a rollback owner and maintenance window are not approved.
Do not execute if alert timestamps are stale or no longer match the active incident.
Do not execute cross-domain changes without NOC and owning team approval.
rollback_or_safety_notes: Capture running configs on all affected devices before any change.
Coordinate maintenance windows across affected sites — changes here have cross-domain blast radius.
Confirm a rollback plan is in place for each affected diagram domain.
Notify NOC before modifying WAN or shared-services components.
Stage changes in the least-critical diagram first; validate before proceeding.
remediation.escalation_recommendation: Escalate to network engineering team if root-cause node cannot be restored within 30 minutes, or if the incident spans more than 3 topology domains. Notify on-call SRE if customer-facing services remain impacted.
